school: Arabic Sufi poetry — the greatest Arabic mystical poet; lived between Cairo and Mecca, his Qasida al-Khamriyya (Wine Ode) and Nazm al-Suluk (Poem of the Way) are the supreme achievements of Arabic Sufi literature
quotes: In my intoxication I do not know whether it is you in me or I in you.
Before the creation of the vine, I was drunk on a wine that was never pressed.
In love, no intermediary stands between the lover and the Beloved.
I drank, and the wine drank me — and the cup passed between us with no witness but Oneness.
My heart beats only to the rhythm of longing.
